title: Bootstrap initialization seed (2026-02)
trigger: Workflow-memory coverage bootstrap for fresh repository/month baseline.
finding: Initialization-only seeded event added to satisfy strict coverage baseline. This does not represent an observed workflow run.
followUp: Continue normal workflow execution and append observed events; keep initialization entries only as baseline markers.
reflection: Initialization seed distinguishes bootstrap coverage from observed operational workflow activity.
feedback: If this command is needed repeatedly, investigate why observed events are not being persisted.

title: best-practice-researcher: meso scan (backend-patterns-effect-use-case-repository)
trigger: Human-requested automation run with playbook weighted random-walk from prior macro tooling baseline
finding: Walk mode=weighted-random; previous path=macro/repo-tooling-and-pipeline-config; chosen path=meso/backend-patterns-effect-use-case-repository; coverage snapshot last8=[macro/repo-tooling-and-pipeline-config, meso/backend-patterns-effect-use-case-repository]; top signal: stale-job recovery path exists but is not wired into worker poll loop despite constants and queue primitive; duplicate-check reused open issues #10 #11 #12 and created #13 for stale recovery; signal score=4/5; followability delta=+2
evidence: Docs compared: AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, agent-engine/workflows/README.md, docs/patterns/use-case.md, docs/patterns/job-queue.md, docs/testing/job-workflow-tests.md, docs/architecture/observability.md; code evidence: apps/worker/src/constants.ts, apps/worker/src/unified-worker.ts, apps/worker/src/base-worker.ts, packages/queue/src/repository.ts, packages/api/src/server/use-cases/runs.ts; duplicate checks: gh issue list --state all --limit 200, gh issue list --search 'stale job failStaleJobs onPollCycle', gh pr list --state open
followUp: Await human triage and ready-for-dev label on #13; continue tracking #10 #11 #12; next walk should stay meso or move micro while avoiding exact path repeat unless unresolved high-priority backend signal warrants revisit
reflection: Reusing existing open issues avoided duplicate backlog while still adding one materially new reliability gap
feedback: When memory add-entry fails from missing dependencies in a new worktree, install before running lane steps to avoid end-of-run interruption
